2F332D6AF876|17|Aldridge forced to sit; tension simmers.

0E258079AE29|20|Silence underscores the impact; room absorbs the horror.

D8628ADDA190|22|Tension eases; reformers gain hope and traction.
EFA56CFA6687|23|Argues the system persisted for convenience, not necessity.

11E42EB84F51|25|Aldridge protests; defensive escalation.

676F71F1A86D|28|Reframes debate: not “did it happen?” but “what now?”
62C18AD36999|29|Lays out the hardliner option: crackdown and suppression.
17EF87F888EA|30|Declares suppression impossible; truth has spread too widely.
59DD614C0506|31|Aldridge challenges with costs and feasibility.
9AB1C9451C61|32|Concrete demands: truth, education, representation, life-extension plan.
6C889C5667AB|33|Hardliners warn of economic/social collapse.
61B9D35CF829|34|Marnette reframes as building a better, human society.
22E0B3176A25|35|Practical objection: colony systems assume a short-lived workforce.
CCD0FC252BCF|36|Proposes collaborative rebuild; elevates salve expertise (Chen).
5E8E81F6750E|37|Raises internal dissent: salves attached to Church/old ways.

EEDEDADAA999|39|Fear question: what if truth sparks violence?
B85AF7BA6E34|40|Marnette advocates honest processing; cites network’s lived practice.
BE191C6B0CE9|41|Personal confession: caregiver work reveals “natural” deaths as engineered murder.
B71B7D83AEB4|42|Moral line in the sand: no more lies, no more scheduled deaths.
704A0D3D8773|43|Silence as climax; weight of history lands.
5D94DF4C6857|44|Feng proposes a Truth & Reconciliation Commission with equal membership.
